The Antonov An-225 Mriya's unparalleled cargo capacity and structural innovations established benchmarks for large-scale aircraft design that continue to influence modern heavy-lift aviation requirements.

The Striking Differences Passengers Notice Between The Airbus A380 & Boeing 747

Find out the key differences between the passenger experience on the Boeing 747 and A380.

Understanding the distinct passenger experience differences between the A380 and 747—such as cabin width, upper deck configurations, and noise levels—helps operators optimize fleet positioning for premium route competitiveness and revenue management.

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space plan debris removal service

Portal Space Systems, a company developing maneuverable spacecraft, is partnering with an Australian startup to offer a commercial orbital debris removal service. The post Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space plan debris removal service appeared first on SpaceNews.

Commercial orbital debris removal services represent an emerging market opportunity for aerospace companies to address the growing congestion in low-Earth orbit while creating new revenue streams.

Commercial Mar 19, 2026

Press Release: British Airways’ first Starlink flight, a fitted 787, takes off

British Airways’ first aircraft equipped with Starlink Wi-Fi took to the skies today, the first flight operated by a UK airline featuring the innovative technology. The 787-8 aircraft bound for Houston is the first in the airline’s fleet to be connected, giving customers free access to fast, reli...

British Airways' deployment of Starlink connectivity on its 787 fleet represents a competitive differentiator in premium long-haul service and signals industry-wide adoption of satellite-based IFE solutions to enhance passenger experience and operational efficiency.

See Runway Girl Network →

Safety Mar 19, 2026

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

A strong safety reporting culture within Safety Management Systems directly correlates with organizational ability to identify and mitigate hazards before they become accidents, making cultural investment essential for measurable safety improvements.

Pilot reported in stable condition following incident under investigation.

Critical engine or system reliability data from this F-35 incident will inform ongoing maintenance protocols and operational procedures across military aviation fleets.

Military Mar 19, 2026

U.S. F-35 Makes Emergency Landing After Allegedly Being Hit by Iranian Fire

CENTCOM says a U.S. F-35 made an emergency landing at a U.S. air base in the Middle East after it “was struck by what is believed to be Iranian fire,” according to sources quoted by CNN. A U.S. F-35 Lightning II fighter jet was forced to make an emergency landing at a U.S. air base […]

The F-35's ability to execute an emergency landing after sustaining combat damage demonstrates the aircraft's structural resilience and pilot recovery capabilities in active conflict zones, with implications for operational readiness and maintenance protocols in contested environments.

See The Aviationist →

First British Airways aircraft with Elon Musk’s Starlink Wi-Fi enters service

The first British Airways aircraft fitted with Elon Musk’s Starlink Wi-Fi has entered service after announcing the plan… The post First British Airways aircraft with Elon Musk’s Starlink Wi-Fi enters service appeared first on AeroTime.

British Airways' adoption of Starlink Wi-Fi represents a significant shift in in-flight connectivity infrastructure, offering airlines a competitive advantage in passenger experience while reducing dependency on traditional air-to-ground systems.

Regulatory Mar 19, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

The 2024 FAA Safety Management Systems rule represents aviation's fundamental shift from investigating accidents after they occur to systematically identifying and mitigating risks before they materialize.

Commercial Mar 19, 2026

Press Release: Egyptair’s new A350-900 to feature Panasonic IFE

Panasonic Avionics Corporation (Panasonic Avionics), the world’s leading supplier of in-flight engagement and connectivity (IFEC) solutions, today announced that EgyptAir’s new A350-900 has entered service featuring award winning in-flight engagement and connectivity (IFEC) systems and a suite of...

EgyptAir's A350-900 deployment with Panasonic's advanced IFEC systems represents a strategic modernization investment that enhances passenger experience and operational competitiveness for carriers competing on long-haul international routes.

Collins' pathway from Air Force test pilot to Space Shuttle Commander demonstrates the critical importance of military aviation expertise and command experience in advancing to leadership roles in human spaceflight operations.

Commercial Mar 19, 2026

Press Release: Tickets on sale for United’s 787-9 with Elevated interior

Starting today, travelers can book seats on United’s most premium international aircraft yet — the 787-9 Dreamliner with the United Elevated interior. Unveiled last May, this new aircraft has been redesigned from nose-to-tail with 99 total premium seats — the highest of percentage among U.S. carr...

United's 787-9 Dreamliner with Elevated interior maximizes premium seat density at 99 seats, representing a strategic competitive differentiation in long-haul international markets where premium cabin revenue is critical.

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

American Bald Eagle at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center

An American bald eagle flies away from its nest and tree at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida on Friday, March 13, 2026. Bald eagle nesting surveys across NASA Kennedy, Merritt Island National Wildlife Refuge, and Canaveral National Seashore are conducted annually to document the number of b...

NASA's Kennedy Space Center's bald eagle nesting program demonstrates how spaceport operations can coexist with wildlife conservation, requiring coordinated environmental stewardship that aviation and aerospace facilities must consider when planning launch activities.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

How NASA is Collecting Explosion Data for Next Generation Rockets

Commercial launch providers continue to advance propulsion technology with a renewed focus on liquid oxygen and methane propelled rockets and spacecraft. As systems grow in scale, carrying millions of pounds of propellant, so too does the responsibility to fully understand the safety profile. Eng...

NASA's systematic collection of explosion data on next-generation methane-oxygen rocket systems is critical for validating safety protocols as commercial launch providers scale propulsion technology to unprecedented fuel capacities.

See NASA →

Industry Mar 19, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization through advanced technologies offers a pathway to simultaneously increase aviation capacity and reduce environmental impact, addressing the industry's critical sustainability challenge as demand continues to grow.

See MDPI →

Etihad Airways Announces Biggest US Expansion In History: 42% Flight Surge

The carrier plans substantially more US flights this summer. See all the developments here!

Etihad's 42% capacity increase to the US signals renewed confidence in transatlantic demand and indicates a strategic pivot toward capturing greater market share in the lucrative North American corridor.

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

NASA Laser Reflecting Instrument Makes GPS Satellite More Accurate

A NASA laser reflecting technology that will aid Global Positioning System (GPS) accuracy is now operational as of March 9. The instrument, known as a laser retroreflector array, or LRA, launched aboard GPS III SV-09, the ninth of U.S. Space Force’s Block III Global Positioning System satellites,...

NASA's laser retroreflector array on GPS III SV-09 enhances positioning accuracy through ground-based laser ranging, providing critical validation and improvement for global navigation systems relied upon by commercial and general aviation operations.

See NASA →

Regulatory Mar 19, 2026

Airlines demand EU leaders set 21-day notice for air traffic control strikes

An airline body representing some of Europe’s largest and most influential carriers is demanding that EU leaders set… The post Airlines demand EU leaders set 21-day notice for air traffic control strikes appeared first on AeroTime.

European airlines are pushing for mandatory strike notice periods to mitigate operational disruptions from air traffic control labor actions, highlighting the critical dependency of commercial aviation on ground infrastructure stability.

See AeroTime Hub →

Regulatory Mar 19, 2026

FCC Exempts Four Drones From Import Ban

March action follows January temporary exemptions tied to broader foreign UAS restrictions.

The FCC's selective drone import exemptions signal a regulatory shift toward case-by-case exceptions to broader foreign UAS restrictions, creating compliance complexity for operators and manufacturers navigating evolving restrictions.

French A400M makes first-ever landing on Arctic sea ice in Greenland

A French Air and Space Force A400M Atlas has landed on Arctic sea ice in northern Greenland, in what France’s… The post French A400M makes first-ever landing on Arctic sea ice in Greenland appeared first on AeroTime.

The A400M's successful Arctic sea ice landing demonstrates the tactical versatility of modern military transport aircraft in extreme environments, expanding operational capabilities for polar logistics and emergency response missions.

Robinson Helicopter Details New Unmanned Business Unit, Cargo UAS Project With Sikorsky

With Robinson Unmanned, […] The post Robinson Helicopter Details New Unmanned Business Unit, Cargo UAS Pr...

Robinson Helicopter's new unmanned business unit and partnership with Sikorsky to develop autonomous cargo helicopters represents a significant diversification strategy addressing the growing demand for unmanned aerial logistics capabilities in commercial operations.

Military Mar 19, 2026

A-10s Now Hunting Iranian Fast Attack Craft in the Strait of Hormuz

U.S. Air Force A-10s in maritime attack role are hunting small Iranian fast attack boats that could threaten shipping or naval forces in the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. Air Force A-10 Thundebolt II jets supporting operation Epic Fury have started flying a different type of mission. As explained by...

The A-10 Thunderbolt II's deployment for maritime patrol operations in the Strait of Hormuz demonstrates the platform's evolving role beyond traditional close air support, with implications for regional force projection and counter-insurgency tactics.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

NASA to Cover Progress 94 Spacecraft Launch, Space Station Docking

NASA will provide live coverage of the launch and docking of a Roscosmos cargo spacecraft carrying about three tons of food, fuel, and supplies for the crew aboard the International Space Station. The unpiloted Roscosmos Progress 94 resupply spacecraft is scheduled to launch at 7:59 a.m. EDT (4:5...

Continued Roscosmos cargo resupply missions to the ISS demonstrate the ongoing operational importance of international partnerships in space logistics, despite geopolitical tensions affecting other aerospace collaborations.

Airbus Built The A350F, But Why Hasn’t Boeing Produced A 787 Freighter?

Boeing is addressing the A350F with another aircraft.

Boeing's strategic decision to compete against Airbus's A350F freighter through an alternative aircraft solution rather than a dedicated 787 freighter variant reflects differing market assessments and operational strategies in the cargo modernization segment.

How Long Did American Airlines Operate The Boeing 747 For?

Find out how long American Airlines flew the Boeing 747 and what led to the early retirement of the iconic “Queen of the Skies.”

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

Meet the Platypi: NASA’s Newest Astronaut Candidate Class

Ten explorers are currently training at NASA’s Johnson Space Center in Houston to become flight-eligible astronauts. Selected in 2025, the astronaut candidates are building the technical and operational skills needed for future missions to the International Space Station, the Moon, and eventually...

NASA's 2025 astronaut candidate class represents the pipeline for sustaining human spaceflight operations across ISS, lunar, and deep-space missions, establishing the workforce foundation for the next decade of aerospace exploration programs.

AeroVironment's $200 million acquisition of ESAero strategically enhances its manufacturing capacity and electric/hybrid UAS capabilities, signaling consolidation in the commercial drone sector to meet growing demand for advanced autonomous systems.

Commercial Mar 19, 2026

How Long Did American Airlines Operate The Boeing 747 For?

Find out how long American Airlines flew the Boeing 747 and what led to the early retirement of the iconic “Queen of the Skies.”

American Airlines' early retirement of the Boeing 747 reflects the broader industry shift toward more fuel-efficient twin-engine widebodies and changing long-haul route economics in the post-9/11 era.

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

Meet the Platypi: NASA’s Newest Astronaut Candidate Class

Ten explorers are currently training at NASA’s Johnson Space Center in Houston to become flight-eligible astronauts. Selected in 2025, the astronaut candidates are building the technical and operational skills needed for future missions to the International Space Station, the Moon, and eventually...

NASA's 2025 astronaut candidate class represents the pipeline for sustaining human spaceflight operations across ISS, lunar, and deep-space missions, establishing the workforce foundation for the next decade of aerospace exploration programs.
